title: Probe repo & decide CLI architecture (framework + HTTP client + entry path)
acceptance_criteria: A written decision (in this task's notes/comments) recording: (1) chosen CLI framework — commander, yargs, or hand-rolled process.argv — based on what is actually resolvable from node_modules (yargs 17 is present transitively; commander is not); (2) chosen HTTP client — built-in fetch (Node>=18), node-fetch, or axios — preferring zero new deps; (3) CLI entry path (e.g. src/cli/index.ts) and where compiled output lands under dist/; (4) how the binary is exposed (package.json bin field name = fleet-e2e-toy) and how npm run build picks it up. No code changes required beyond optional documentation; the decision unblocks downstream tasks.

title: Final quality gate: lint + build + test all green
acceptance_criteria: npm run lint exits 0 with no errors. npm run build exits 0 and produces dist/cli/index.js. npm test exits 0 with all suites passing including the new CLI tests. No unused exports, no any types in new src/cli/ code (interfaces live in src/models/ or src/cli/types.ts).

title: Add CLI tests (arg parsing, validation, help, version, subcommand happy paths)
acceptance_criteria: tests/cli.test.ts (Jest) covers: (a) --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0 and exits 0; (b) --help and -h exit 0 and include 'Usage'; (c) missing required --id on read/update/delete exits non-zero with 'error:' on stderr and no 'at ' stack frames; (d) empty/whitespace --title on create rejected with non-zero exit; (e) at least one subcommand happy path against the existing Express app — start it in-process on an ephemeral port (or use supertest indirectly by importing app and a fetch-against-app shim) and exercise list+create+read+delete. All tests pass under npm test.

title: Wire --version / -V flag (gh-toy-4ef)
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy --version and fleet-e2e-toy -V both print exactly 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' followed by a newline to stdout and exit 0. The flag is recognized before subcommand dispatch (so it works on its own). Combination with --help is consistent (whichever is parsed first wins; both still exit 0). Version string is sourced from package.json version field (read at build/runtime) so it stays in sync.

title: Wire global and per-subcommand --help / -h (gh-toy-7rp help portion)
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy --help and -h print top-level usage (program name, version line, list of subcommands with one-line descriptions, and the env var NOTEAPI_URL hint) to stdout and exit 0. fleet-e2e-toy <subcommand> --help (and -h) print subcommand-specific usage with all flags listed and required/optional markers, then exit 0. fleet-e2e-toy with no args prints usage to stderr and exits non-zero. Unknown subcommand prints 'error: unknown command <x>' on stderr plus usage, exits non-zero. No stack traces anywhere in help/error output.

title: Implement CLI 'delete' subcommand
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy delete --id <id> calls DELETE /api/notes/:id. --id required and non-empty. On 204 prints 'deleted <id>' to stdout, exits 0. On 404 prints 'error: Note not found' to stderr, exits non-zero. No stack traces.

title: Implement CLI 'update' subcommand
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy update --id <id> [--title <t>] [--content <c>] calls PUT /api/notes/:id. --id required and non-empty; at least one of --title or --content must be present (otherwise validation error). Each provided field validated non-empty. On success prints updated note id to stdout, exits 0. API 404 surfaces as 'error: Note not found' on stderr, non-zero exit. No stack traces.

title: Implement CLI 'create' subcommand
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy create --title <t> --content <c> [--tag <t1> --tag <t2> ...] calls POST /api/notes. --title and --content required and validated via shared helpers (reject empty/whitespace). On success prints the created note id (and title) to stdout and exits 0. On API 400 prints 'error: <server validation message>' to stderr and exits non-zero. No stack traces.

title: Implement CLI 'read' subcommand
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy read --id <id> calls GET /api/notes/:id and prints the note to stdout (human-readable: id, title, tags, createdAt, then content). --id is required; missing/empty --id rejected via validation helpers with clear error and non-zero exit. 404 from API surfaces as 'error: Note not found' on stderr with non-zero exit. No stack traces in error output.

title: Implement CLI 'list' subcommand
acceptance_criteria: fleet-e2e-toy list calls GET /api/notes via the api-client helper. Supports optional --tag <value> and --q <value> flags (both passed through as query params). Prints results to stdout as a human-readable table or one note per line including id and title; if NOTEAPI_JSON=1 or --json is supported (optional, not required), prints raw JSON. Empty input strings for --tag/--q rejected via shared validation helpers. Exits 0 on success, non-zero on API error.

title: Add shared CLI input validation helpers (covers gh-toy-7rp validation portion)
acceptance_criteria: src/cli/validation.ts exports helpers such as requireNonEmptyString(name, value) and requireDefined(name, value) that throw a typed CliValidationError with a clear, single-line message when value is undefined, not a string, empty, or whitespace-only. A top-level error handler in src/cli/index.ts catches CliValidationError, prints 'error: <message>' to stderr (NO stack trace), and exits with code 2. Generic Error instances also have stack traces suppressed in the printed output. Unit tests under tests/cli-validation.test.ts cover the empty/whitespace/missing cases.

title: Add HTTP client helper for NoteAPI calls
acceptance_criteria: src/cli/api-client.ts exports typed helpers (listNotes, getNote, createNote, updateNote, deleteNote) that call the NoteAPI using the HTTP client chosen in T1. Base URL comes from process.env.NOTEAPI_URL with default http://localhost:3000. Each helper returns parsed JSON on 2xx; on non-2xx it throws an Error with a message of the form 'API error <status>: <server message or text>' (no raw error objects). Reuses Note / CreateNoteInput / UpdateNoteInput types from src/models/note.ts. npm run build succeeds.

title: Scaffold CLI entry point and wire build
acceptance_criteria: src/cli/index.ts exists with a top-level shebang (#!/usr/bin/env node), parses process.argv (or uses chosen framework from T1) and dispatches to subcommand stubs (list/read/create/update/delete) that currently just print 'not implemented yet' to stderr and exit 1. tsconfig already includes src/** so npm run build emits dist/cli/index.js. package.json gains a bin entry mapping fleet-e2e-toy -> dist/cli/index.js. npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors. Running node dist/cli/index.js with no args prints a usage line and exits non-zero.

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: Add input validation for empty or blank strings
description: When user passes empty string or whitespace-only string as argument, reject with clear error message. Non-zero exit code. Add unit test. Ref: gh-toy-v6z.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.

title: Add config file support (~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml)
description: Allow users to persist default flag values in a YAML config file at ~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml so they don't have to repeat common flags on every run. Acceptance: reads config on startup, CLI flags override config, warns on unknown keys, documents supported keys in README.

title: Handle SIGINT gracefully (Ctrl-C)
description: The tool should catch SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and exit cleanly without a Python traceback or incomplete output. Acceptance: Ctrl-C prints 'Interrupted.' and exits with code 130, no stack trace shown, partial output files cleaned up.

title: Add JSON output mode via --json flag
description: Add a --json flag so all command output can be emitted as machine-readable JSON for scripting and CI pipelines. Acceptance: --json flag accepted on any subcommand, output is valid JSON, human-readable output is default, errors also JSON-formatted.

title: Tag filtering endpoint
description: GET /api/notes?tag=work returns only notes with that tag. Already partially implemented - needs tests.
